@@ -0,0 +1,24 @@
+---
+depends_on:
+- 01KWMEEA0FQB66C11H0V13TGR4
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: 8b80
+title: 'Examples: Librarian, BigCatalog, and HotReload executables'
+---
+## What
+Build the remaining three `Examples/` executable targets per plan.md §13 (M8):
+- `Examples/Librarian/main.swift` — selection mode end-to-end on a Router model: cached root seeded with a catalog, fork per query, ids-only xgrammar-constrained selection, verbatim blocks out; intent-level queries ("the warmest city on my trip" → `tripCities` + `weather`). Runs locally against a tiny `mlx-community` model (the gated-integration setup).
+- `Examples/BigCatalog/main.swift` — the headroom story: synthetic ~10³-entry catalog (ids = URIs), in-memory retrieval with printed timings, then a selection query that overflows the budget → top-M → one-off session with the `onRetrievalCut` diagnostic printed.
+- `Examples/HotReload/main.swift` — `update(items:)` bursts (MCP-style add/remove): immediate keyword searchability, async embed catch-up progress, root + grammar rebuild shown.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] All three targets compile as part of `swift build` (CI keeps them compiling)
+- [ ] `swift run BigCatalog` completes GPU-free through its retrieval half and prints timings; the selection half runs when a model is available
+- [ ] `swift run Librarian` and `swift run HotReload` run end-to-end locally with the tiny-model setup
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `Tests/FoundationModelsMetadataRegistryTests/ExamplesSmokeTests.swift` — extend with the GPU-free portions (BigCatalog retrieval timing path, HotReload index-rebuild path with a FakeEmbedder)
+- [ ] Run `swift build && swift test` — exit 0 without GPU
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

-- `Examples/BigCatalog/main.swift` — the headroom story: synthetic ~10³-entry catalog (ids = URIs), in-memory retrieval with printed timings, then a selection query that overflows the budget → top-M → one-off session with the `onRetrievalCut` diagnostic printed.

+Build the remaining three `Examples/` executable targets per plan.md §13 (M8), extending the `Package.swift` example-target setup and `ExamplesSmokeTests.swift` created by the CatalogSearch/SemanticSearch task:
+- `Examples/Librarian/main.swift` — selection mode end-to-end on a Router model: cached root seeded with a catalog, fork per query, ids-only xgrammar-constrained selection, verbatim blocks out; intent-level queries ("the warmest city on my trip" → `tripCities` + `weather`). The model run is behind the **same opt-in env var as the gated integration suite**; without it the example prints its catalog and exits 0.
+- `Examples/BigCatalog/main.swift` — the headroom story: synthetic ~10³-entry catalog (ids = URIs), in-memory retrieval with printed timings (GPU-free), then — only when the env var is set — a selection query that overflows the budget → top-M → one-off session with the `.retrievalCut` diagnostic printed.
+- `Examples/HotReload/main.swift` — `update(items:)` bursts (MCP-style add/remove): immediate keyword searchability, embed catch-up progress via `.embedCatchUp`, root + grammar rebuild shown; runs GPU-free with a `FakeEmbedder`-style deterministic embedder, real model only behind the env var.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] All three targets compile as part of `swift build` (CI keeps them compiling)
-- [ ] `swift run BigCatalog` completes GPU-free through its retrieval half and prints timings; the selection half runs when a model is available
-- [ ] `swift run Librarian` and `swift run HotReload` run end-to-end locally with the tiny-model setup
+- [ ] All three targets compile as part of `swift build` (CI keeps them compiling via the scaffold task's workflow)
+- [ ] `swift run BigCatalog` (no env var) exits 0 GPU-free and prints retrieval timings
+- [ ] `swift run Librarian` and `swift run HotReload` (no env var) exit 0 GPU-free on their degraded paths; with the gated-integration env var set they run the full model-backed flow locally

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `Tests/FoundationModelsMetadataRegistryTests/ExamplesSmokeTests.swift` — extend with the GPU-free portions (BigCatalog retrieval timing path, HotReload index-rebuild path with a FakeEmbedder)
+- [ ] `Tests/FoundationModelsMetadataRegistryTests/ExamplesSmokeTests.swift` — extend with the GPU-free portions (BigCatalog retrieval timing path, HotReload index-rebuild path with a deterministic embedder), asserting exit/output of the factored entry functions
 - [ ] Run `swift build && swift test` — exit 0 without GPU

 ## Workflow

+  id: 01kwq237gfyck2xx5y0jghvpfz
+  text: |-
+    Implementation complete, build/test green. Summary:
+
+    - Package.swift: added LibrarianCore/Librarian, BigCatalogCore/BigCatalog, HotReloadCore/HotReload target pairs (mirroring the CatalogSearchCore/SemanticSearchCore *Core-library-plus-thin-executable pattern), and added BigCatalogCore + HotReloadCore to the test target's dependencies (LibrarianCore deliberately NOT added -- its only GPU-free behavior is the catalog print, which needs no dedicated unit test per the task's explicit Tests scope).
+    - Examples/ExamplesSupport/ExamplesSupport.swift: generalized `formattedMatches` from `[Match<GitCommand>]` to `<Item: SearchableMetadata>(matches: [Match<Item>])` so all five examples share one formatter; added `metadataRegistryIntegrationEnvVar`/`metadataRegistryIntegrationEnabled` (reads "METADATA_REGISTRY_INTEGRATION_TESTS", the exact literal ^3ncv5a3 established) since all three new examples needed the same gate check (rule-of-three: shared rather than duplicated 3x).
+    - Examples/LibrarianCore + Examples/Librarian: trip-planning catalog (tripCities/weather/currency/packingList/flightStatus), `.selection` mode end-to-end -- cached root + fork-per-call (catalog deliberately kept under SelectionConfig's default budget), ids-only xgrammar-constrained via a hand-built JSON Schema (see design note below), intent-level query "the warmest city on my trip". Gated behind the shared env var; ungated path just prints the catalog and exits 0. No new unit tests (task's Tests section didn't ask for any; its only GPU-free behavior is trivial catalog printing).
+    - Examples/BigCatalogCore + Examples/BigCatalog: `makeBigCatalog(count:)` synthetic ~1000-entry catalog (ids = URIs) with one deterministic "needle" entry; `runBigCatalogRetrieval` GPU-free retrieval-timing core (tested); gated `runBigCatalogOverBudgetSelection` forces the over-budget top-M + one-off session path via a deliberately tiny `capacityCharacterLimit`, printing `.retrievalCut`.
+    - Examples/HotReloadCore + Examples/HotReload: `DeterministicEmbedder` (GPU-free FakeEmbedder-style double, byte-hash + L2-normalize), `runHotReloadBurst` replays an MCP-style add/remove burst against a live `MetadataSearcher`, capturing per-step diagnostics (`.embedCatchUp`) and immediate keyword-search results (tested); `runSelectionRootRebuildDemo` shows the selection tier's cached-root/grammar rebuild on a real catalog change via a scripted `AgentSession` double (tested). Gated `resolveLiveEmbedder` replays the same burst against a real embedder.
+    - Tests/.../ExamplesSmokeTests.swift: added 4 new GPU-free tests (BigCatalog retrieval-timing + formatter, HotReload burst-staging + selection-root-rebuild), following the established `@testable import *Core` pattern -- watched them fail to compile first (target had no source files), then implemented until green.
+
+    Design note: `SelectionTier.idEnumGrammar(ids:)` is package-internal, so LibrarianCore/BigCatalogCore each hand-build an equivalent JSON Schema locally (`{"ids": {"type":"array","items":{"enum":[...]}, "uniqueItems":true}}`) rather than reaching into the package's internals or depending on `Selection.generationSchema`'s cross-module accessibility (unverified) -- this is what a real integrator outside the package would do, and `MetadataSearcher`'s selection tier still filters/reports any out-of-candidate id regardless of how the grammar was built.
+
+    Verification (all fresh, no METADATA_REGISTRY_INTEGRATION_TESTS set, no model downloads):
+    - `swift build`: exit 0, no new warnings (only the pre-existing unrelated mlx-swift bundle warning).
+    - `swift test`: 130/130 passed (126 prior + 4 new), gated RouterIntegrationTests suite correctly skipped.
+    - `swift run BigCatalog` / `swift run Librarian` / `swift run HotReload`: all exit 0 on GPU-free/ungated paths with correct output.
+
+    Adversarial double-check review in progress via really-done.

+## What
Build the remaining three `Examples/` executable targets per plan.md §13 (M8), extending the `Package.swift` example-target setup and `ExamplesSmokeTests.swift` created by the CatalogSearch/SemanticSearch task:
- `Examples/Librarian/main.swift` — selection mode end-to-end on a Router model: cached root seeded with a catalog, fork per query, ids-only xgrammar-constrained selection, verbatim blocks out; intent-level queries ("the warmest city on my trip" → `tripCities` + `weather`). The model run is behind the **same opt-in env var as the gated integration suite**; without it the example prints its catalog and exits 0.
- `Examples/BigCatalog/main.swift` — the headroom story: synthetic ~10³-entry catalog (ids = URIs), in-memory retrieval with printed timings (GPU-free), then — only when the env var is set — a selection query that overflows the budget → top-M → one-off session with the `.retrievalCut` diagnostic printed.
- `Examples/HotReload/main.swift` — `update(items:)` bursts (MCP-style add/remove): immediate keyword searchability, embed catch-up progress via `.embedCatchUp`, root + grammar rebuild shown; runs GPU-free with a `FakeEmbedder`-style deterministic embedder, real model only behind the env var.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] All three targets compile as part of `swift build` (CI keeps them compiling via the scaffold task's workflow)
- [x] `swift run BigCatalog` (no env var) exits 0 GPU-free and prints retrieval timings
- [x] `swift run Librarian` and `swift run HotReload` (no env var) exit 0 GPU-free on their degraded paths; with the gated-integration env var set they run the full model-backed flow locally

## Tests
- [x] `Tests/FoundationModelsMetadataRegistryTests/ExamplesSmokeTests.swift` — extend with the GPU-free portions (BigCatalog retrieval timing path, HotReload index-rebuild path with a deterministic embedder), asserting exit/output of the factored entry functions
- [x] Run `swift build && swift test` — exit 0 without GPU

## Workflow
- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.

## Review Findings (2026-07-04 12:37)

- [ ] `Examples/BigCatalogCore/BigCatalogCore.swift:15` — BigCatalogItem duplicates the SearchableMetadata fixture pattern (id + description property + init + renderBlock). Differs from GitCommand only in struct name and property name. Extract to shared type (see ExamplesSupport.swift:13).
- [ ] `Examples/BigCatalogCore/BigCatalogCore.swift:169` — runBigCatalogOverBudgetSelection() inlines the same SelectionConfig-building pattern that is extracted as resolveLiveSelectionConfig() in LibrarianCore. Both call resolveLiveProfile, idEnumGrammar, then create a SelectionConfig with an identical model factory. Differ only in parameters and the addition of capacityCharacterLimit. Extract to shared helper in LiveRouterSupport parameterized by id set and optional capacity limit (see LibrarianCore.swift:93).
- [ ] `Examples/BigCatalogCore/BigCatalogCore.swift:173` — printDiagnostic() follows the exact same pattern as SemanticSearchCore.printDiagnostic(): check for a specific diagnostic case, print a custom message, fall back to MetadataDiagnostic.log(). Differ only in which diagnostic case is checked (.retrievalCut vs .embeddingUnavailable) and what message is printed. This is a 'one function with arguments' case. Extract a shared helper parameterized by the diagnostic case matcher and message text, or use a closure to handle case-specific behavior.
- [ ] `Examples/ExamplesSupport/ExamplesSupport.swift:13` — GitCommand struct establishes a pattern replicated verbatim three more times. All four SearchableMetadata types (GitCommand, BigCatalogItem, HotReloadTool, TripPlanningTool) are identical except for struct name and the property name for the description field (summary vs block). These are one type parameterized by name. Extract a single generic SearchableMetadata type or create a macro/builder that both reduces duplication and makes future fixture types trivial to add.
- [ ] `Examples/HotReloadCore/HotReloadCore.swift:66` — Public property `dimension` lacks documentation. It is a public member of a public struct but has no doc comment explaining its purpose or relationship to the embedder's output. Add a doc comment above the property explaining what the dimension represents and its default value, e.g. `/// The length of every embedding vector this embedder produces.`.
- [ ] `Examples/HotReloadCore/HotReloadCore.swift:76` — Public method `embed(_:)` lacks documentation. It is a public protocol conformance method on a public struct but has no doc comment explaining its behavior or parameters. Add a doc comment documenting the method's parameters, return value, and deterministic hashing behavior, e.g. `/// Embeds the given texts into deterministic vectors using a pure hash function.` with parameter/return documentation.
- [ ] `Examples/HotReloadCore/HotReloadCore.swift:101` — `public func embed` is a public member of a public struct but carries no `///` doc comment. Every public declaration must be documented. Add a documentation comment above `public func embed` describing what it does, its parameters, return value, and any throws. Example: `/// Embeds the given texts into deterministic vectors.` with a `- Parameters:` block for `texts` and a `- Returns:` line.
- [ ] `Examples/HotReloadCore/HotReloadCore.swift:309` — resolveLiveEmbedder() is a near-verbatim duplicate of SemanticSearchCore.resolveLiveEmbedder(). Both have identical structure and only differ in parameter values. Extract to shared helper function parameterized by demoLabel, name, and description (see SemanticSearchCore.swift:85).
- [ ] `Examples/HotReloadCore/HotReloadCore.swift:314` — resolveLiveEmbedder reimplements SemanticSearchCore.resolveLiveEmbedder with identical logic, differing only in parameters (demoLabel, name, description). Both should call a single shared function in LiveRouterSupport rather than maintaining duplicates. Extract a shared resolveLiveEmbedder(demoLabel:name:description:) function to LiveRouterSupport.swift that accepts these three parameters, then call it from both SemanticSearchCore and HotReloadCore with their respective values.
- [ ] `Examples/LibrarianCore/LibrarianCore.swift:17` — TripPlanningTool is a verbatim duplicate of the GitCommand/BigCatalogItem/HotReloadTool pattern, differing only in struct name and property name. Extract to shared type (see ExamplesSupport.swift:13).
- [ ] `Examples/LibrarianCore/LibrarianCore.swift:93` — resolveLiveSelectionConfig() implements the same SelectionConfig-building pattern that is also inlined in BigCatalogCore.runBigCatalogOverBudgetSelection(). Both call resolveLiveProfile, then idEnumGrammar, then create a SelectionConfig with an identical model factory closure. Differ only in the parameters to resolveLiveProfile, the id set for idEnumGrammar, and (in BigCatalog) an additional capacityCharacterLimit. This is a 'one function with arguments' case. Extract a shared helper in LiveRouterSupport (e.g., `buildSelectionConfig(ids: [String], capacityCharacterLimit: Int?) async throws -> SelectionConfig`) parameterized by the id set and optional capacity limit.
- [ ] `Examples/SemanticSearchCore/SemanticSearchCore.swift:85` — resolveLiveEmbedder() is a near-verbatim copy of HotReloadCore.resolveLiveEmbedder(). The entire function body is identical except for three parameter strings passed to resolveLiveProfile (demoLabel, name, description). This is a 'one function with arguments' case. Extract a shared helper function parameterized by demoLabel, name, and description: `func buildEmbedderAdapter(demoLabel: String, name: String, description: String) async throws -> any TextEmbedding`.
- [ ] `Examples/SemanticSearchCore/SemanticSearchCore.swift:100` — printDiagnostic() is a near-verbatim duplicate of BigCatalogCore.printDiagnostic(). Both follow identical if-case/else-log pattern, differing only in the diagnostic case checked and the message printed. Extract to shared helper function parameterized by diagnostic case and message (see BigCatalogCore.swift:173).

\ No newline at end of file

- [ ] `Examples/BigCatalogCore/BigCatalogCore.swift:173` — printDiagnostic() follows the exact same pattern as SemanticSearchCore.printDiagnostic(): check for a specific diagnostic case, print a custom message, fall back to MetadataDiagnostic.log(). Extract a shared helper parameterized by the diagnostic case matcher and message text, or use a closure to handle case-specific behavior.
- [ ] `Examples/ExamplesSupport/ExamplesSupport.swift:13` — GitCommand struct establishes a pattern replicated verbatim three more times. All four SearchableMetadata types (GitCommand, BigCatalogItem, HotReloadTool, TripPlanningTool) are identical except for struct name and the property name for the description field (summary vs block). These are one type parameterized by name. Extract a single generic SearchableMetadata type or create a macro/builder that both reduces duplication and makes future fixture types trivial to add.
- [ ] `Examples/HotReloadCore/HotReloadCore.swift:66` — Public property `dimension` lacks documentation. It is a public member of a public struct but has no doc comment explaining its purpose or relationship to the embedder's output. - [ ] `Examples/HotReloadCore/HotReloadCore.swift:76` — Public method `embed(_:)` lacks documentation. It is a public protocol conformance method on a public struct but has no doc comment explaining its behavior or parameters. Add a doc comment documenting the method's parameters, return value, and deterministic hashing behavior, e.g. `/// Embeds the given texts into deterministic vectors using a pure hash function.` with parameter/return documentation.
- [ ] `Examples/HotReloadCore/HotReloadCore.swift:101` — `public func embed` is a public member of a public struct but carries no `///` doc comment. Every public declaration must be documented. Add a documentation comment above `public func embed` describing what it does, its parameters, return value, and any throws. Example: `/// Embeds the given texts into deterministic vectors.` with a `- Parameters:` block for `texts` and a `- Returns:` line.
- [ ] `Examples/HotReloadCore/HotReloadCore.swift:309` — resolveLiveEmbedder() is a near-verbatim duplicate of SemanticSearchCore.resolveLiveEmbedder(). Both have identical structure and only differ in parameter values. Extract to shared helper function parameterized by demoLabel, name, and description (see SemanticSearchCore.swift:85).
- [ ] `Examples/HotReloadCore/HotReloadCore.swift:314` — resolveLiveEmbedder reimplements SemanticSearchCore.resolveLiveEmbedder with identical logic, differing only in parameters (demoLabel, name, description). Both should call a single shared function in LiveRouterSupport rather than maintaining duplicates. Extract a shared resolveLiveEmbedder(demoLabel:name:description:) function to LiveRouterSupport.swift that accepts these three parameters, then call it from both SemanticSearchCore and HotReloadCore with their respective values.
- [ ] `Examples/LibrarianCore/LibrarianCore.swift:17` — TripPlanningTool is a verbatim duplicate of the GitCommand/BigCatalogItem/HotReloadTool pattern, differing only in struct name and property name. Extract to shared type (see ExamplesSupport.swift:13).
- [ ] `Examples/LibrarianCore/LibrarianCore.swift:93` — resolveLiveSelectionConfig() implements the same SelectionConfig-building pattern that is also inlined in BigCatalogCore.runBigCatalogOverBudgetSelection(). Both call resolveLiveProfile, then idEnumGrammar, then create a SelectionConfig with an identical model factory closure. Differ only in the parameters to resolveLiveProfile, the id set for idEnumGrammar, and (in BigCatalog) an additional capacityCharacterLimit. This is a 'one function with arguments' case. Extract a shared helper in LiveRouterSupport (e.g., `buildSelectionConfig(ids: [String], capacityCharacterLimit: Int?) async throws -> SelectionConfig`) parameterized by the id set and optional capacity limit.
- [ ] `Examples/SemanticSearchCore/SemanticSearchCore.swift:85` — resolveLiveEmbedder() is a near-verbatim copy of HotReloadCore.resolveLiveEmbedder(). The entire function body is identical except for three parameter strings passed to resolveLiveProfile (demoLabel, name, description). This is a 'one function with arguments' case. Extract a shared helper function parameterized by demoLabel, name, and description: `func buildEmbedderAdapter(demoLabel: String, name: String, description: String) async throws -> any TextEmbedding`.
- [ ] `Examples/SemanticSearchCore/SemanticSearchCore.swift:100` — printDiagnostic() is a near-verbatim duplicate of BigCatalogCore.printDiagnostic(). Both follow identical if-case/else-log pattern, differing only in the diagnostic case checked and the message printed. Extract to shared helper function parameterized by diagnostic case and message (see BigCatalogCore.swift:173).

-## What
Build the remaining three `Examples/` executable targets per plan.md §13 (M8), extending the `Package.swift` example-target setup and `ExamplesSmokeTests.swift` created by the CatalogSearch/SemanticSearch task:
- `Examples/Librarian/main.swift` — selection mode end-to-end on a Router model: cached root seeded with a catalog, fork per query, ids-only xgrammar-constrained selection, verbatim blocks out; intent-level queries ("the warmest city on my trip" → `tripCities` + `weather`). The model run is behind the **same opt-in env var as the gated integration suite**; without it the example prints its catalog and exits 0.
- `Examples/BigCatalog/main.swift` — the headroom story: synthetic ~10³-entry catalog (ids = URIs), in-memory retrieval with printed timings (GPU-free), then — only when the env var is set — a selection query that overflows the budget → top-M → one-off session with the `.retrievalCut` diagnostic printed.
- `Examples/HotReload/main.swift` — `update(items:)` bursts (MCP-style add/remove): immediate keyword searchability, embed catch-up progress via `.embedCatchUp`, root + grammar rebuild shown; runs GPU-free with a `FakeEmbedder`-style deterministic embedder, real model only behind the env var.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] All three targets compile as part of `swift build` (CI keeps them compiling via the scaffold task's workflow)
- [x] `swift run BigCatalog` (no env var) exits 0 GPU-free and prints retrieval timings
- [x] `swift run Librarian` and `swift run HotReload` (no env var) exit 0 GPU-free on their degraded paths; with the gated-integration env var set they run the full model-backed flow locally

## Tests
- [x] `Tests/FoundationModelsMetadataRegistryTests/ExamplesSmokeTests.swift` — extend with the GPU-free portions (BigCatalog retrieval timing path, HotReload index-rebuild path with a deterministic embedder), asserting exit/output of the factored entry functions
- [x] Run `swift build && swift test` — exit 0 without GPU

## Workflow
- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.

## Review Findings (2026-07-04 12:37)

- [x] `Examples/BigCatalogCore/BigCatalogCore.swift:15` — BigCatalogItem duplicates the SearchableMetadata fixture pattern (id + description property + init + renderBlock). Differs from GitCommand only in struct name and property name. Extract to shared type (see ExamplesSupport.swift:13).
- [x] `Examples/BigCatalogCore/BigCatalogCore.swift:169` — runBigCatalogOverBudgetSelection() inlines the same SelectionConfig-building pattern that is extracted as resolveLiveSelectionConfig() in LibrarianCore. Both call resolveLiveProfile, idEnumGrammar, then create a SelectionConfig with an identical model factory. Differ only in parameters and the addition of capacityCharacterLimit. Extract to shared helper in LiveRouterSupport parameterized by id set and optional capacity limit (see LibrarianCore.swift:93).
- [x] `Examples/BigCatalogCore/BigCatalogCore.swift:173` — printDiagnostic() follows the exact same pattern as SemanticSearchCore.printDiagnostic(): check for a specific diagnostic case, print a custom message, fall back to MetadataDiagnostic.log(). Differ only in which diagnostic case is checked (.retrievalCut vs .embeddingUnavailable) and what message is printed. This is a 'one function with arguments' case. Extract a shared helper parameterized by the diagnostic case matcher and message text, or use a closure to handle case-specific behavior.
- [x] `Examples/ExamplesSupport/ExamplesSupport.swift:13` — GitCommand struct establishes a pattern replicated verbatim three more times. All four SearchableMetadata types (GitCommand, BigCatalogItem, HotReloadTool, TripPlanningTool) are identical except for struct name and the property name for the description field (summary vs block). These are one type parameterized by name. Extract a single generic SearchableMetadata type or create a macro/builder that both reduces duplication and makes future fixture types trivial to add.
- [x] `Examples/HotReloadCore/HotReloadCore.swift:66` — Public property `dimension` lacks documentation. It is a public member of a public struct but has no doc comment explaining its purpose or relationship to the embedder's output. Add a doc comment above the property explaining what the dimension represents and its default value, e.g. `/// The length of every embedding vector this embedder produces.`.
- [x] `Examples/HotReloadCore/HotReloadCore.swift:76` — Public method `embed(_:)` lacks documentation. It is a public protocol conformance method on a public struct but has no doc comment explaining its behavior or parameters. Add a doc comment documenting the method's parameters, return value, and deterministic hashing behavior, e.g. `/// Embeds the given texts into deterministic vectors using a pure hash function.` with parameter/return documentation.
- [x] `Examples/HotReloadCore/HotReloadCore.swift:101` — `public func embed` is a public member of a public struct but carries no `///` doc comment. Every public declaration must be documented. Add a documentation comment above `public func embed` describing what it does, its parameters, return value, and any throws. Example: `/// Embeds the given texts into deterministic vectors.` with a `- Parameters:` block for `texts` and a `- Returns:` line.
- [x] `Examples/HotReloadCore/HotReloadCore.swift:309` — resolveLiveEmbedder() is a near-verbatim duplicate of SemanticSearchCore.resolveLiveEmbedder(). Both have identical structure and only differ in parameter values. Extract to shared helper function parameterized by demoLabel, name, and description (see SemanticSearchCore.swift:85).
- [x] `Examples/HotReloadCore/HotReloadCore.swift:314` — resolveLiveEmbedder reimplements SemanticSearchCore.resolveLiveEmbedder with identical logic, differing only in parameters (demoLabel, name, description). Both should call a single shared function in LiveRouterSupport rather than maintaining duplicates. Extract a shared resolveLiveEmbedder(demoLabel:name:description:) function to LiveRouterSupport.swift that accepts these three parameters, then call it from both SemanticSearchCore and HotReloadCore with their respective values.
- [x] `Examples/LibrarianCore/LibrarianCore.swift:17` — TripPlanningTool is a verbatim duplicate of the GitCommand/BigCatalogItem/HotReloadTool pattern, differing only in struct name and property name. Extract to shared type (see ExamplesSupport.swift:13).
- [x] `Examples/LibrarianCore/LibrarianCore.swift:93` — resolveLiveSelectionConfig() implements the same SelectionConfig-building pattern that is also inlined in BigCatalogCore.runBigCatalogOverBudgetSelection(). Both call resolveLiveProfile, then idEnumGrammar, then create a SelectionConfig with an identical model factory closure. Differ only in the parameters to resolveLiveProfile, the id set for idEnumGrammar, and (in BigCatalog) an additional capacityCharacterLimit. This is a 'one function with arguments' case. Extract a shared helper in LiveRouterSupport (e.g., `buildSelectionConfig(ids: [String], capacityCharacterLimit: Int?) async throws -> SelectionConfig`) parameterized by the id set and optional capacity limit.
- [x] `Examples/SemanticSearchCore/SemanticSearchCore.swift:85` — resolveLiveEmbedder() is a near-verbatim copy of HotReloadCore.resolveLiveEmbedder(). The entire function body is identical except for three parameter strings passed to resolveLiveProfile (demoLabel, name, description). This is a 'one function with arguments' case. Extract a shared helper function parameterized by demoLabel, name, and description: `func buildEmbedderAdapter(demoLabel: String, name: String, description: String) async throws -> any TextEmbedding`.
- [x] `Examples/SemanticSearchCore/SemanticSearchCore.swift:100` — printDiagnostic() is a near-verbatim duplicate of BigCatalogCore.printDiagnostic(). Both follow identical if-case/else-log pattern, differing only in the diagnostic case checked and the message printed. Extract to shared helper function parameterized by diagnostic case and message (see BigCatalogCore.swift:173).

-## What
Build the remaining three `Examples/` executable targets per plan.md §13 (M8), extending the `Package.swift` example-target setup and `ExamplesSmokeTests.swift` created by the CatalogSearch/SemanticSearch task:
- `Examples/Librarian/main.swift` — selection mode end-to-end on a Router model: cached root seeded with a catalog, fork per query, ids-only xgrammar-constrained selection, verbatim blocks out; intent-level queries ("the warmest city on my trip" → `tripCities` + `weather`). The model run is behind the **same opt-in env var as the gated integration suite**; without it the example prints its catalog and exits 0.
- `Examples/BigCatalog/main.swift` — the headroom story: synthetic ~10³-entry catalog (ids = URIs), in-memory retrieval with printed timings (GPU-free), then — only when the env var is set — a selection query that overflows the budget → top-M → one-off session with the `.retrievalCut` diagnostic printed.
- `Examples/HotReload/main.swift` — `update(items:)` bursts (MCP-style add/remove): immediate keyword searchability, embed catch-up progress via `.embedCatchUp`, root + grammar rebuild shown; runs GPU-free with a `FakeEmbedder`-style deterministic embedder, real model only behind the env var.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] All three targets compile as part of `swift build` (CI keeps them compiling via the scaffold task's workflow)
- [x] `swift run BigCatalog` (no env var) exits 0 GPU-free and prints retrieval timings
- [x] `swift run Librarian` and `swift run HotReload` (no env var) exit 0 GPU-free on their degraded paths; with the gated-integration env var set they run the full model-backed flow locally

## Tests
- [x] `Tests/FoundationModelsMetadataRegistryTests/ExamplesSmokeTests.swift` — extend with the GPU-free portions (BigCatalog retrieval timing path, HotReload index-rebuild path with a deterministic embedder), asserting exit/output of the factored entry functions
- [x] Run `swift build && swift test` — exit 0 without GPU

## Workflow
- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.

## Review Findings (2026-07-04 12:37)

- [x] `Examples/BigCatalogCore/BigCatalogCore.swift:15` — BigCatalogItem duplicates the SearchableMetadata fixture pattern (id + description property + init + renderBlock). Differs from GitCommand only in struct name and property name. Extract to shared type (see ExamplesSupport.swift:13).
- [x] `Examples/BigCatalogCore/BigCatalogCore.swift:169` — runBigCatalogOverBudgetSelection() inlines the same SelectionConfig-building pattern that is extracted as resolveLiveSelectionConfig() in LibrarianCore. Both call resolveLiveProfile, idEnumGrammar, then create a SelectionConfig with an identical model factory. Differ only in parameters and the addition of capacityCharacterLimit. Extract to shared helper in LiveRouterSupport parameterized by id set and optional capacity limit (see LibrarianCore.swift:93).
- [x] `Examples/BigCatalogCore/BigCatalogCore.swift:173` — printDiagnostic() follows the exact same pattern as SemanticSearchCore.printDiagnostic(): check for a specific diagnostic case, print a custom message, fall back to MetadataDiagnostic.log(). Differ only in which diagnostic case is checked (.retrievalCut vs .embeddingUnavailable) and what message is printed. This is a 'one function with arguments' case. Extract a shared helper parameterized by the diagnostic case matcher and message text, or use a closure to handle case-specific behavior.
- [x] `Examples/ExamplesSupport/ExamplesSupport.swift:13` — GitCommand struct establishes a pattern replicated verbatim three more times. All four SearchableMetadata types (GitCommand, BigCatalogItem, HotReloadTool, TripPlanningTool) are identical except for struct name and the property name for the description field (summary vs block). These are one type parameterized by name. Extract a single generic SearchableMetadata type or create a macro/builder that both reduces duplication and makes future fixture types trivial to add.
- [x] `Examples/HotReloadCore/HotReloadCore.swift:66` — Public property `dimension` lacks documentation. It is a public member of a public struct but has no doc comment explaining its purpose or relationship to the embedder's output. Add a doc comment above the property explaining what the dimension represents and its default value, e.g. `/// The length of every embedding vector this embedder produces.`.
- [x] `Examples/HotReloadCore/HotReloadCore.swift:76` — Public method `embed(_:)` lacks documentation. It is a public protocol conformance method on a public struct but has no doc comment explaining its behavior or parameters. Add a doc comment documenting the method's parameters, return value, and deterministic hashing behavior, e.g. `/// Embeds the given texts into deterministic vectors using a pure hash function.` with parameter/return documentation.
- [x] `Examples/HotReloadCore/HotReloadCore.swift:101` — `public func embed` is a public member of a public struct but carries no `///` doc comment. Every public declaration must be documented. Add a documentation comment above `public func embed` describing what it does, its parameters, return value, and any throws. Example: `/// Embeds the given texts into deterministic vectors.` with a `- Parameters:` block for `texts` and a `- Returns:` line.
- [x] `Examples/HotReloadCore/HotReloadCore.swift:309` — resolveLiveEmbedder() is a near-verbatim duplicate of SemanticSearchCore.resolveLiveEmbedder(). Both have identical structure and only differ in parameter values. Extract to shared helper function parameterized by demoLabel, name, and description (see SemanticSearchCore.swift:85).
- [x] `Examples/HotReloadCore/HotReloadCore.swift:314` — resolveLiveEmbedder reimplements SemanticSearchCore.resolveLiveEmbedder with identical logic, differing only in parameters (demoLabel, name, description). Both should call a single shared function in LiveRouterSupport rather than maintaining duplicates. Extract a shared resolveLiveEmbedder(demoLabel:name:description:) function to LiveRouterSupport.swift that accepts these three parameters, then call it from both SemanticSearchCore and HotReloadCore with their respective values.
- [x] `Examples/LibrarianCore/LibrarianCore.swift:17` — TripPlanningTool is a verbatim duplicate of the GitCommand/BigCatalogItem/HotReloadTool pattern, differing only in struct name and property name. Extract to shared type (see ExamplesSupport.swift:13).
- [x] `Examples/LibrarianCore/LibrarianCore.swift:93` — resolveLiveSelectionConfig() implements the same SelectionConfig-building pattern that is also inlined in BigCatalogCore.runBigCatalogOverBudgetSelection(). Both call resolveLiveProfile, then idEnumGrammar, then create a SelectionConfig with an identical model factory closure. Differ only in the parameters to resolveLiveProfile, the id set for idEnumGrammar, and (in BigCatalog) an additional capacityCharacterLimit. This is a 'one function with arguments' case. Extract a shared helper in LiveRouterSupport (e.g., `buildSelectionConfig(ids: [String], capacityCharacterLimit: Int?) async throws -> SelectionConfig`) parameterized by the id set and optional capacity limit.
- [x] `Examples/SemanticSearchCore/SemanticSearchCore.swift:85` — resolveLiveEmbedder() is a near-verbatim copy of HotReloadCore.resolveLiveEmbedder(). The entire function body is identical except for three parameter strings passed to resolveLiveProfile (demoLabel, name, description). This is a 'one function with arguments' case. Extract a shared helper function parameterized by demoLabel, name, and description: `func buildEmbedderAdapter(demoLabel: String, name: String, description: String) async throws -> any TextEmbedding`.
- [x] `Examples/SemanticSearchCore/SemanticSearchCore.swift:100` — printDiagnostic() is a near-verbatim duplicate of BigCatalogCore.printDiagnostic(). Both follow identical if-case/else-log pattern, differing only in the diagnostic case checked and the message printed. Extract to shared helper function parameterized by diagnostic case and message (see BigCatalogCore.swift:173).

Per the rule, non-mutating Boolean members should read as assertions about state (e.g., `isEmpty`, `isEnabled`), not bare or unqualified adjectives (`empty`, `enabled`). - [ ] `Package.swift:193` — SemanticSearchCore target's dependencies list is identical to BigCatalogCore, LibrarianCore, and HotReloadCore. This repeated target structure appears 5 times and should be generated from a helper function or data structure. This repeated target structure appears 5 times and should be generated from a helper function or data structure. Extract to shared helper function as described above.
- [ ] `Package.swift:239` — BigCatalogCore target's dependencies list is identical to SemanticSearchCore, LibrarianCore, and HotReloadCore. +## What
Build the remaining three `Examples/` executable targets per plan.md §13 (M8), extending the `Package.swift` example-target setup and `ExamplesSmokeTests.swift` created by the CatalogSearch/SemanticSearch task:
- `Examples/Librarian/main.swift` — selection mode end-to-end on a Router model: cached root seeded with a catalog, fork per query, ids-only xgrammar-constrained selection, verbatim blocks out; intent-level queries ("the warmest city on my trip" → `tripCities` + `weather`). The model run is behind the **same opt-in env var as the gated integration suite**; without it the example prints its catalog and exits 0.
- `Examples/BigCatalog/main.swift` — the headroom story: synthetic ~10³-entry catalog (ids = URIs), in-memory retrieval with printed timings (GPU-free), then — only when the env var is set — a selection query that overflows the budget → top-M → one-off session with the `.retrievalCut` diagnostic printed.
- `Examples/HotReload/main.swift` — `update(items:)` bursts (MCP-style add/remove): immediate keyword searchability, embed catch-up progress via `.embedCatchUp`, root + grammar rebuild shown; runs GPU-free with a `FakeEmbedder`-style deterministic embedder, real model only behind the env var.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] All three targets compile as part of `swift build` (CI keeps them compiling via the scaffold task's workflow)
- [x] `swift run BigCatalog` (no env var) exits 0 GPU-free and prints retrieval timings
- [x] `swift run Librarian` and `swift run HotReload` (no env var) exit 0 GPU-free on their degraded paths; with the gated-integration env var set they run the full model-backed flow locally

## Tests
- [x] `Tests/FoundationModelsMetadataRegistryTests/ExamplesSmokeTests.swift` — extend with the GPU-free portions (BigCatalog retrieval timing path, HotReload index-rebuild path with a deterministic embedder), asserting exit/output of the factored entry functions
- [x] Run `swift build && swift test` — exit 0 without GPU

## Workflow
- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.

## Review Findings (2026-07-04 12:37)

- [x] `Examples/BigCatalogCore/BigCatalogCore.swift:15` — BigCatalogItem duplicates the SearchableMetadata fixture pattern (id + description property + init + renderBlock). Differs from GitCommand only in struct name and property name. Extract to shared type (see ExamplesSupport.swift:13).
- [x] `Examples/BigCatalogCore/BigCatalogCore.swift:169` — runBigCatalogOverBudgetSelection() inlines the same SelectionConfig-building pattern that is extracted as resolveLiveSelectionConfig() in LibrarianCore. Both call resolveLiveProfile, idEnumGrammar, then create a SelectionConfig with an identical model factory. Differ only in parameters and the addition of capacityCharacterLimit. Extract to shared helper in LiveRouterSupport parameterized by id set and optional capacity limit (see LibrarianCore.swift:93).
- [x] `Examples/BigCatalogCore/BigCatalogCore.swift:173` — printDiagnostic() follows the exact same pattern as SemanticSearchCore.printDiagnostic(): check for a specific diagnostic case, print a custom message, fall back to MetadataDiagnostic.log(). Differ only in which diagnostic case is checked (.retrievalCut vs .embeddingUnavailable) and what message is printed. This is a 'one function with arguments' case. Extract a shared helper parameterized by the diagnostic case matcher and message text, or use a closure to handle case-specific behavior.
- [x] `Examples/ExamplesSupport/ExamplesSupport.swift:13` — GitCommand struct establishes a pattern replicated verbatim three more times. All four SearchableMetadata types (GitCommand, BigCatalogItem, HotReloadTool, TripPlanningTool) are identical except for struct name and the property name for the description field (summary vs block). These are one type parameterized by name. Extract a single generic SearchableMetadata type or create a macro/builder that both reduces duplication and makes future fixture types trivial to add.
- [x] `Examples/HotReloadCore/HotReloadCore.swift:66` — Public property `dimension` lacks documentation. It is a public member of a public struct but has no doc comment explaining its purpose or relationship to the embedder's output. Add a doc comment above the property explaining what the dimension represents and its default value, e.g. `/// The length of every embedding vector this embedder produces.`.
- [x] `Examples/HotReloadCore/HotReloadCore.swift:76` — Public method `embed(_:)` lacks documentation. It is a public protocol conformance method on a public struct but has no doc comment explaining its behavior or parameters. Add a doc comment documenting the method's parameters, return value, and deterministic hashing behavior, e.g. `/// Embeds the given texts into deterministic vectors using a pure hash function.` with parameter/return documentation.
- [x] `Examples/HotReloadCore/HotReloadCore.swift:101` — `public func embed` is a public member of a public struct but carries no `///` doc comment. Every public declaration must be documented. Add a documentation comment above `public func embed` describing what it does, its parameters, return value, and any throws. Example: `/// Embeds the given texts into deterministic vectors.` with a `- Parameters:` block for `texts` and a `- Returns:` line.
- [x] `Examples/HotReloadCore/HotReloadCore.swift:309` — resolveLiveEmbedder() is a near-verbatim duplicate of SemanticSearchCore.resolveLiveEmbedder(). Both have identical structure and only differ in parameter values. Extract to shared helper function parameterized by demoLabel, name, and description (see SemanticSearchCore.swift:85).
- [x] `Examples/HotReloadCore/HotReloadCore.swift:314` — resolveLiveEmbedder reimplements SemanticSearchCore.resolveLiveEmbedder with identical logic, differing only in parameters (demoLabel, name, description). Both should call a single shared function in LiveRouterSupport rather than maintaining duplicates. Extract a shared resolveLiveEmbedder(demoLabel:name:description:) function to LiveRouterSupport.swift that accepts these three parameters, then call it from both SemanticSearchCore and HotReloadCore with their respective values.
- [x] `Examples/LibrarianCore/LibrarianCore.swift:17` — TripPlanningTool is a verbatim duplicate of the GitCommand/BigCatalogItem/HotReloadTool pattern, differing only in struct name and property name. Extract to shared type (see ExamplesSupport.swift:13).
- [x] `Examples/LibrarianCore/LibrarianCore.swift:93` — resolveLiveSelectionConfig() implements the same SelectionConfig-building pattern that is also inlined in BigCatalogCore.runBigCatalogOverBudgetSelection(). - [x] `Examples/SemanticSearchCore/SemanticSearchCore.swift:85` — resolveLiveEmbedder() is a near-verbatim copy of HotReloadCore.resolveLiveEmbedder(). The entire function body is identical except for three parameter strings passed to resolveLiveProfile (demoLabel, name, description). This is a 'one function with arguments' case. Extract a shared helper function parameterized by demoLabel, name, and description: `func buildEmbedderAdapter(demoLabel: String, name: String, description: String) async throws -> any TextEmbedding`.
- [x] `Examples/SemanticSearchCore/SemanticSearchCore.swift:100` — printDiagnostic() is a near-verbatim duplicate of BigCatalogCore.printDiagnostic(). Both follow identical if-case/else-log pattern, differing only in the diagnostic case checked and the message printed. Extract to shared helper function parameterized by diagnostic case and message (see BigCatalogCore.swift:173).

-## What
Build the remaining three `Examples/` executable targets per plan.md §13 (M8), extending the `Package.swift` example-target setup and `ExamplesSmokeTests.swift` created by the CatalogSearch/SemanticSearch task:
- `Examples/Librarian/main.swift` — selection mode end-to-end on a Router model: cached root seeded with a catalog, fork per query, ids-only xgrammar-constrained selection, verbatim blocks out; intent-level queries ("the warmest city on my trip" → `tripCities` + `weather`). The model run is behind the **same opt-in env var as the gated integration suite**; without it the example prints its catalog and exits 0.
- `Examples/BigCatalog/main.swift` — the headroom story: synthetic ~10³-entry catalog (ids = URIs), in-memory retrieval with printed timings (GPU-free), then — only when the env var is set — a selection query that overflows the budget → top-M → one-off session with the `.retrievalCut` diagnostic printed.
- `Examples/HotReload/main.swift` — `update(items:)` bursts (MCP-style add/remove): immediate keyword searchability, embed catch-up progress via `.embedCatchUp`, root + grammar rebuild shown; runs GPU-free with a `FakeEmbedder`-style deterministic embedder, real model only behind the env var.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] All three targets compile as part of `swift build` (CI keeps them compiling via the scaffold task's workflow)
- [x] `swift run BigCatalog` (no env var) exits 0 GPU-free and prints retrieval timings
- [x] `swift run Librarian` and `swift run HotReload` (no env var) exit 0 GPU-free on their degraded paths; with the gated-integration env var set they run the full model-backed flow locally

## Tests
- [x] `Tests/FoundationModelsMetadataRegistryTests/ExamplesSmokeTests.swift` — extend with the GPU-free portions (BigCatalog retrieval timing path, HotReload index-rebuild path with a deterministic embedder), asserting exit/output of the factored entry functions
- [x] Run `swift build && swift test` — exit 0 without GPU

## Workflow
- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.

-## What
Build the remaining three `Examples/` executable targets per plan.md §13 (M8), extending the `Package.swift` example-target setup and `ExamplesSmokeTests.swift` created by the CatalogSearch/SemanticSearch task:
- `Examples/Librarian/main.swift` — selection mode end-to-end on a Router model: cached root seeded with a catalog, fork per query, ids-only xgrammar-constrained selection, verbatim blocks out; intent-level queries ("the warmest city on my trip" → `tripCities` + `weather`). The model run is behind the **same opt-in env var as the gated integration suite**; without it the example prints its catalog and exits 0.
- `Examples/BigCatalog/main.swift` — the headroom story: synthetic ~10³-entry catalog (ids = URIs), in-memory retrieval with printed timings (GPU-free), then — only when the env var is set — a selection query that overflows the budget → top-M → one-off session with the `.retrievalCut` diagnostic printed.
- `Examples/HotReload/main.swift` — `update(items:)` bursts (MCP-style add/remove): immediate keyword searchability, embed catch-up progress via `.embedCatchUp`, root + grammar rebuild shown; runs GPU-free with a `FakeEmbedder`-style deterministic embedder, real model only behind the env var.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] All three targets compile as part of `swift build` (CI keeps them compiling via the scaffold task's workflow)
- [x] `swift run BigCatalog` (no env var) exits 0 GPU-free and prints retrieval timings
- [x] `swift run Librarian` and `swift run HotReload` (no env var) exit 0 GPU-free on their degraded paths; with the gated-integration env var set they run the full model-backed flow locally

## Tests
- [x] `Tests/FoundationModelsMetadataRegistryTests/ExamplesSmokeTests.swift` — extend with the GPU-free portions (BigCatalog retrieval timing path, HotReload index-rebuild path with a deterministic embedder), asserting exit/output of the factored entry functions
- [x] Run `swift build && swift test` — exit 0 without GPU

## Workflow
- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.

-## What
Build the remaining three `Examples/` executable targets per plan.md §13 (M8), extending the `Package.swift` example-target setup and `ExamplesSmokeTests.swift` created by the CatalogSearch/SemanticSearch task:
- `Examples/Librarian/main.swift` — selection mode end-to-end on a Router model: cached root seeded with a catalog, fork per query, ids-only xgrammar-constrained selection, verbatim blocks out; intent-level queries ("the warmest city on my trip" → `tripCities` + `weather`). The model run is behind the **same opt-in env var as the gated integration suite**; without it the example prints its catalog and exits 0.
- `Examples/BigCatalog/main.swift` — the headroom story: synthetic ~10³-entry catalog (ids = URIs), in-memory retrieval with printed timings (GPU-free), then — only when the env var is set — a selection query that overflows the budget → top-M → one-off session with the `.retrievalCut` diagnostic printed.
- `Examples/HotReload/main.swift` — `update(items:)` bursts (MCP-style add/remove): immediate keyword searchability, embed catch-up progress via `.embedCatchUp`, root + grammar rebuild shown; runs GPU-free with a `FakeEmbedder`-style deterministic embedder, real model only behind the env var.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] All three targets compile as part of `swift build` (CI keeps them compiling via the scaffold task's workflow)
- [x] `swift run BigCatalog` (no env var) exits 0 GPU-free and prints retrieval timings
- [x] `swift run Librarian` and `swift run HotReload` (no env var) exit 0 GPU-free on their degraded paths; with the gated-integration env var set they run the full model-backed flow locally

## Tests
- [x] `Tests/FoundationModelsMetadataRegistryTests/ExamplesSmokeTests.swift` — extend with the GPU-free portions (BigCatalog retrieval timing path, HotReload index-rebuild path with a deterministic embedder), asserting exit/output of the factored entry functions
- [x] Run `swift build && swift test` — exit 0 without GPU

## Workflow
- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.

Extract to shared helper in LiveRouterSupport parameterized by id set and optional capacity limit (see LibrarianCore.swift:93).
- [x] `Examples/BigCatalogCore/BigCatalogCore.swift:173` — printDiagnostic() follows the exact same pattern as SemanticSearchCore.printDiagnostic(): check for a specific diagnostic case, print a custom message, fall back to MetadataDiagnostic.log(). Differ only in which diagnostic case is checked (.retrievalCut vs .embeddingUnavailable) and what message is printed. This is a 'one function with arguments' case. Extract a shared helper parameterized by the diagnostic case matcher and message text, or use a closure to handle case-specific behavior.
- [x] `Examples/ExamplesSupport/ExamplesSupport.swift:13` — GitCommand struct establishes a pattern replicated verbatim three more times. All four SearchableMetadata types (GitCommand, BigCatalogItem, HotReloadTool, TripPlanningTool) are identical except for struct name and the property name for the description field (summary vs block). These are one type parameterized by name. Extract a single generic SearchableMetadata type or create a macro/builder that both reduces duplication and makes future fixture types trivial to add.
- [x] `Examples/HotReloadCore/HotReloadCore.swift:66` — Public property `dimension` lacks documentation. It is a public member of a public struct but has no doc comment explaining its purpose or relationship to the embedder's output. +## What
Build the remaining three `Examples/` executable targets per plan.md §13 (M8), extending the `Package.swift` example-target setup and `ExamplesSmokeTests.swift` created by the CatalogSearch/SemanticSearch task:
- `Examples/Librarian/main.swift` — selection mode end-to-end on a Router model: cached root seeded with a catalog, fork per query, ids-only xgrammar-constrained selection, verbatim blocks out; intent-level queries ("the warmest city on my trip" → `tripCities` + `weather`). The model run is behind the **same opt-in env var as the gated integration suite**; without it the example prints its catalog and exits 0.
- `Examples/BigCatalog/main.swift` — the headroom story: synthetic ~10³-entry catalog (ids = URIs), in-memory retrieval with printed timings (GPU-free), then — only when the env var is set — a selection query that overflows the budget → top-M → one-off session with the `.retrievalCut` diagnostic printed.
- `Examples/HotReload/main.swift` — `update(items:)` bursts (MCP-style add/remove): immediate keyword searchability, embed catch-up progress via `.embedCatchUp`, root + grammar rebuild shown; runs GPU-free with a `FakeEmbedder`-style deterministic embedder, real model only behind the env var.

-## What
Build the remaining three `Examples/` executable targets per plan.md §13 (M8), extending the `Package.swift` example-target setup and `ExamplesSmokeTests.swift` created by the CatalogSearch/SemanticSearch task:
- `Examples/Librarian/main.swift` — selection mode end-to-end on a Router model: cached root seeded with a catalog, fork per query, ids-only xgrammar-constrained selection, verbatim blocks out; intent-level queries ("the warmest city on my trip" → `tripCities` + `weather`). The model run is behind the **same opt-in env var as the gated integration suite**; without it the example prints its catalog and exits 0.
- `Examples/BigCatalog/main.swift` — the headroom story: synthetic ~10³-entry catalog (ids = URIs), in-memory retrieval with printed timings (GPU-free), then — only when the env var is set — a selection query that overflows the budget → top-M → one-off session with the `.retrievalCut` diagnostic printed.
- `Examples/HotReload/main.swift` — `update(items:)` bursts (MCP-style add/remove): immediate keyword searchability, embed catch-up progress via `.embedCatchUp`, root + grammar rebuild shown; runs GPU-free with a `FakeEmbedder`-style deterministic embedder, real model only behind the env var.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] All three targets compile as part of `swift build` (CI keeps them compiling via the scaffold task's workflow)
- [x] `swift run BigCatalog` (no env var) exits 0 GPU-free and prints retrieval timings
- [x] `swift run Librarian` and `swift run HotReload` (no env var) exit 0 GPU-free on their degraded paths; with the gated-integration env var set they run the full model-backed flow locally

## Tests
- [x] `Tests/FoundationModelsMetadataRegistryTests/ExamplesSmokeTests.swift` — extend with the GPU-free portions (BigCatalog retrieval timing path, HotReload index-rebuild path with a deterministic embedder), asserting exit/output of the factored entry functions
- [x] Run `swift build && swift test` — exit 0 without GPU

## Workflow
- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.

## Review Findings (2026-07-04 12:37)

- [x] `Examples/BigCatalogCore/BigCatalogCore.swift:15` — BigCatalogItem duplicates the SearchableMetadata fixture pattern (id + description property + init + renderBlock). Differs from GitCommand only in struct name and property name. Extract to shared type (see ExamplesSupport.swift:13).
- [x] `Examples/BigCatalogCore/BigCatalogCore.swift:169` — runBigCatalogOverBudgetSelection() inlines the same SelectionConfig-building pattern that is extracted as resolveLiveSelectionConfig() in LibrarianCore. Both call resolveLiveProfile, idEnumGrammar, then create a SelectionConfig with an identical model factory. Differ only in parameters and the addition of capacityCharacterLimit. Extract to shared helper in LiveRouterSupport parameterized by id set and optional capacity limit (see LibrarianCore.swift:93).
- [x] `Examples/BigCatalogCore/BigCatalogCore.swift:173` — printDiagnostic() follows the exact same pattern as SemanticSearchCore.printDiagnostic(): check for a specific diagnostic case, print a custom message, fall back to MetadataDiagnostic.log(). Differ only in which diagnostic case is checked (.retrievalCut vs .embeddingUnavailable) and what message is printed. This is a 'one function with arguments' case. Extract a shared helper parameterized by the diagnostic case matcher and message text, or use a closure to handle case-specific behavior.
- [x] `Examples/ExamplesSupport/ExamplesSupport.swift:13` — GitCommand struct establishes a pattern replicated verbatim three more times. All four SearchableMetadata types (GitCommand, BigCatalogItem, HotReloadTool, TripPlanningTool) are identical except for struct name and the property name for the description field (summary vs block). These are one type parameterized by name. Extract a single generic SearchableMetadata type or create a macro/builder that both reduces duplication and makes future fixture types trivial to add.
- [x] `Examples/HotReloadCore/HotReloadCore.swift:66` — Public property `dimension` lacks documentation. It is a public member of a public struct but has no doc comment explaining its purpose or relationship to the embedder's output. Add a doc comment above the property explaining what the dimension represents and its default value, e.g. `/// The length of every embedding vector this embedder produces.`.
- [x] `Examples/HotReloadCore/HotReloadCore.swift:76` — Public method `embed(_:)` lacks documentation. It is a public protocol conformance method on a public struct but has no doc comment explaining its behavior or parameters. Add a doc comment documenting the method's parameters, return value, and deterministic hashing behavior, e.g. `/// Embeds the given texts into deterministic vectors using a pure hash function.` with parameter/return documentation.
- [x] `Examples/HotReloadCore/HotReloadCore.swift:101` — `public func embed` is a public member of a public struct but carries no `///` doc comment. Every public declaration must be documented. Add a documentation comment above `public func embed` describing what it does, its parameters, return value, and any throws. Example: `/// Embeds the given texts into deterministic vectors.` with a `- Parameters:` block for `texts` and a `- Returns:` line.
- [x] `Examples/HotReloadCore/HotReloadCore.swift:309` — resolveLiveEmbedder() is a near-verbatim duplicate of SemanticSearchCore.resolveLiveEmbedder(). Both have identical structure and only differ in parameter values. Extract to shared helper function parameterized by demoLabel, name, and description (see SemanticSearchCore.swift:85).
- [x] `Examples/HotReloadCore/HotReloadCore.swift:314` — resolveLiveEmbedder reimplements SemanticSearchCore.resolveLiveEmbedder with identical logic, differing only in parameters (demoLabel, name, description). Both should call a single shared function in LiveRouterSupport rather than maintaining duplicates. Extract a shared resolveLiveEmbedder(demoLabel:name:description:) function to LiveRouterSupport.swift that accepts these three parameters, then call it from both SemanticSearchCore and HotReloadCore with their respective values.
- [x] `Examples/LibrarianCore/LibrarianCore.swift:17` — TripPlanningTool is a verbatim duplicate of the GitCommand/BigCatalogItem/HotReloadTool pattern, differing only in struct name and property name. Extract to shared type (see ExamplesSupport.swift:13).
- [x] `Examples/LibrarianCore/LibrarianCore.swift:93` — resolveLiveSelectionConfig() implements the same SelectionConfig-building pattern that is also inlined in BigCatalogCore.runBigCatalogOverBudgetSelection(). Both call resolveLiveProfile, then idEnumGrammar, then create a SelectionConfig with an identical model factory closure. Differ only in the parameters to resolveLiveProfile, the id set for idEnumGrammar, and (in BigCatalog) an additional capacityCharacterLimit. This is a 'one function with arguments' case. Extract a shared helper in LiveRouterSupport (e.g., `buildSelectionConfig(ids: [String], capacityCharacterLimit: Int?) async throws -> SelectionConfig`) parameterized by the id set and optional capacity limit.
- [x] `Examples/SemanticSearchCore/SemanticSearchCore.swift:85` — resolveLiveEmbedder() is a near-verbatim copy of HotReloadCore.resolveLiveEmbedder(). The entire function body is identical except for three parameter strings passed to resolveLiveProfile (demoLabel, name, description). This is a 'one function with arguments' case. Extract a shared helper function parameterized by demoLabel, name, and description: `func buildEmbedderAdapter(demoLabel: String, name: String, description: String) async throws -> any TextEmbedding`.
- [x] `Examples/SemanticSearchCore/SemanticSearchCore.swift:100` — printDiagnostic() is a near-verbatim duplicate of BigCatalogCore.printDiagnostic(). Both follow identical if-case/else-log pattern, differing only in the diagnostic case checked and the message printed. Extract to shared helper function parameterized by diagnostic case and message (see BigCatalogCore.swift:173).

## Review Findings (2026-07-04 13:04)

- [x] `Examples/ExamplesSupport/ExamplesSupport.swift:77` — Boolean property should use `is` prefix for standard Swift naming convention. Per the rule, non-mutating Boolean members should read as assertions about state (e.g., `isEmpty`, `isEnabled`), not bare or unqualified adjectives (`empty`, `enabled`). Rename to `isMetadataRegistryIntegrationEnabled`.
- [x] `Examples/HotReloadCore/HotReloadCore.swift:14` — Public constant `hotReloadToolB` is missing a doc comment. Per the documentation rule, every public declaration must carry a `///` doc comment (except overrides). Add a doc comment immediately before `hotReloadToolB`, e.g., `/// The second tool in the burst sequence.`.
- [x] `Package.swift:193` — SemanticSearchCore target's dependencies list is identical to BigCatalogCore, LibrarianCore, and HotReloadCore. This repeated configuration spans 8 identical dependency entries and should be extracted to a constant to prevent future drift. Extract to a constant before the targets array: `let gateLiveExamplesDependencies = [.target(name: packageName), .target(name: "ExamplesSupport"), .target(name: "LiveRouterSupport"), .product(name: routerDependencyName, package: routerDependencyName), .product(name: "MLXHuggingFace", package: mlxPackage), .product(name: "MLXLMCommon", package: mlxPackage), .product(name: "HuggingFace", package: huggingFacePackage), .product(name: "Tokenizers", package: transformersPackage)]` and use `dependencies: gateLiveExamplesDependencies` in each target.
- [x] `Package.swift:216` — CatalogSearch executable target has an identical dependency/path pattern to SemanticSearch, Librarian, BigCatalog, and HotReload: each is an .executableTarget with [.target(name: "*Core"), .target(name: "ExamplesSupport")] and path "Examples/*". This repeated target structure appears 5 times and should be generated from a helper function or data structure. Extract a helper function: `func exampleExecutableTarget(name: String, coreName: String) -> Target { .executableTarget(name: name, dependencies: [.target(name: coreName), .target(name: "ExamplesSupport")], path: "Examples/\(name)") }` and replace each .executableTarget with a call to this function.
- [x] `Package.swift:226` — SemanticSearch executable target has an identical dependency/path pattern to CatalogSearch, Librarian, BigCatalog, and HotReload. This repeated target structure appears 5 times and should be generated from a helper function or data structure. Extract to shared helper function as described above.
- [x] `Package.swift:239` — BigCatalogCore target's dependencies list is identical to SemanticSearchCore, LibrarianCore, and HotReloadCore. This repeated configuration spans 8 identical dependency entries and should be extracted to a constant to prevent future drift. Extract to a shared constant `gateLiveExamplesDependencies` as noted above and reuse in this target.
- [x] `Package.swift:258` — LibrarianCore target's dependencies list is identical to SemanticSearchCore, BigCatalogCore, and HotReloadCore. This repeated configuration spans 8 identical dependency entries and should be extracted to a constant to prevent future drift. Extract to a shared constant `gateLiveExamplesDependencies` as noted above and reuse in this target.
- [x] `Package.swift:268` — Librarian executable target has an identical dependency/path pattern to CatalogSearch, SemanticSearch, BigCatalog, and HotReload. This repeated target structure appears 5 times and should be generated from a helper function or data structure. Extract to shared helper function as described above.
- [x] `Package.swift:300` — HotReloadCore target's dependencies list is identical to SemanticSearchCore, BigCatalogCore, and LibrarianCore. This repeated configuration spans 8 identical dependency entries and should be extracted to a constant to prevent future drift. Extract to a shared constant `gateLiveExamplesDependencies` as noted above and reuse in this target.
- [x] `Package.swift:312` — HotReload executable target has an identical dependency/path pattern to CatalogSearch, SemanticSearch, Librarian, and BigCatalog. This repeated target structure appears 5 times and should be generated from a helper function or data structure. Extract to shared helper function as described above.

## Review Findings (2026-07-04 13:24)

- [x] `Package.swift:54` — The 5-entry product dependency list (routerDependencyName, MLXHuggingFace, MLXLMCommon, HuggingFace, Tokenizers) appears identically in three separate places, violating the rule that repeated configuration should be a named constant. Maintenance of this list requires updates in three locations instead of one. Extract the 5 products as a shared constant (`let liveRouterProductDependencies: [Target.Dependency] = [...]`) and reuse it across all three locations to maintain a single source of truth.

+## What
Build the remaining three `Examples/` executable targets per plan.md §13 (M8), extending the `Package.swift` example-target setup and `ExamplesSmokeTests.swift` created by the CatalogSearch/SemanticSearch task:
- `Examples/Librarian/main.swift` — selection mode end-to-end on a Router model: cached root seeded with a catalog, fork per query, ids-only xgrammar-constrained selection, verbatim blocks out; intent-level queries ("the warmest city on my trip" → `tripCities` + `weather`). The model run is behind the **same opt-in env var as the gated integration suite**; without it the example prints its catalog and exits 0.
- `Examples/BigCatalog/main.swift` — the headroom story: synthetic ~10³-entry catalog (ids = URIs), in-memory retrieval with printed timings (GPU-free), then — only when the env var is set — a selection query that overflows the budget → top-M → one-off session with the `.retrievalCut` diagnostic printed.
- `Examples/HotReload/main.swift` — `update(items:)` bursts (MCP-style add/remove): immediate keyword searchability, embed catch-up progress via `.embedCatchUp`, root + grammar rebuild shown; runs GPU-free with a `FakeEmbedder`-style deterministic embedder, real model only behind the env var.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] All three targets compile as part of `swift build` (CI keeps them compiling via the scaffold task's workflow)
- [x] `swift run BigCatalog` (no env var) exits 0 GPU-free and prints retrieval timings
- [x] `swift run Librarian` and `swift run HotReload` (no env var) exit 0 GPU-free on their degraded paths; with the gated-integration env var set they run the full model-backed flow locally

## Tests
- [x] `Tests/FoundationModelsMetadataRegistryTests/ExamplesSmokeTests.swift` — extend with the GPU-free portions (BigCatalog retrieval timing path, HotReload index-rebuild path with a deterministic embedder), asserting exit/output of the factored entry functions
- [x] Run `swift build && swift test` — exit 0 without GPU

## Workflow
- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.

-## What
Build the remaining three `Examples/` executable targets per plan.md §13 (M8), extending the `Package.swift` example-target setup and `ExamplesSmokeTests.swift` created by the CatalogSearch/SemanticSearch task:
- `Examples/Librarian/main.swift` — selection mode end-to-end on a Router model: cached root seeded with a catalog, fork per query, ids-only xgrammar-constrained selection, verbatim blocks out; intent-level queries ("the warmest city on my trip" → `tripCities` + `weather`). The model run is behind the **same opt-in env var as the gated integration suite**; without it the example prints its catalog and exits 0.
- `Examples/BigCatalog/main.swift` — the headroom story: synthetic ~10³-entry catalog (ids = URIs), in-memory retrieval with printed timings (GPU-free), then — only when the env var is set — a selection query that overflows the budget → top-M → one-off session with the `.retrievalCut` diagnostic printed.
- `Examples/HotReload/main.swift` — `update(items:)` bursts (MCP-style add/remove): immediate keyword searchability, embed catch-up progress via `.embedCatchUp`, root + grammar rebuild shown; runs GPU-free with a `FakeEmbedder`-style deterministic embedder, real model only behind the env var.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] All three targets compile as part of `swift build` (CI keeps them compiling via the scaffold task's workflow)
- [x] `swift run BigCatalog` (no env var) exits 0 GPU-free and prints retrieval timings
- [x] `swift run Librarian` and `swift run HotReload` (no env var) exit 0 GPU-free on their degraded paths; with the gated-integration env var set they run the full model-backed flow locally

## Tests
- [x] `Tests/FoundationModelsMetadataRegistryTests/ExamplesSmokeTests.swift` — extend with the GPU-free portions (BigCatalog retrieval timing path, HotReload index-rebuild path with a deterministic embedder), asserting exit/output of the factored entry functions
- [x] Run `swift build && swift test` — exit 0 without GPU

## Workflow
- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.

 ## What
Build the remaining three `Examples/` executable targets per plan.md §13 (M8), extending the `Package.swift` example-target setup and `ExamplesSmokeTests.swift` created by the CatalogSearch/SemanticSearch task:
- `Examples/Librarian/main.swift` — selection mode end-to-end on a Router model: cached root seeded with a catalog, fork per query, ids-only xgrammar-constrained selection, verbatim blocks out; intent-level queries ("the warmest city on my trip" → `tripCities` + `weather`). The model run is behind the **same opt-in env var as the gated integration suite**; without it the example prints its catalog and exits 0.
- `Examples/BigCatalog/main.swift` — the headroom story: synthetic ~10³-entry catalog (ids = URIs), in-memory retrieval with printed timings (GPU-free), then — only when the env var is set — a selection query that overflows the budget → top-M → one-off session with the `.retrievalCut` diagnostic printed.
- `Examples/HotReload/main.swift` — `update(items:)` bursts (MCP-style add/remove): immediate keyword searchability, embed catch-up progress via `.embedCatchUp`, root + grammar rebuild shown; runs GPU-free with a `FakeEmbedder`-style deterministic embedder, real model only behind the env var.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] All three targets compile as part of `swift build` (CI keeps them compiling via the scaffold task's workflow)
- [x] `swift run BigCatalog` (no env var) exits 0 GPU-free and prints retrieval timings
- [x] `swift run Librarian` and `swift run HotReload` (no env var) exit 0 GPU-free on their degraded paths; with the gated-integration env var set they run the full model-backed flow locally

## Tests
- [x] `Tests/FoundationModelsMetadataRegistryTests/ExamplesSmokeTests.swift` — extend with the GPU-free portions (BigCatalog retrieval timing path, HotReload index-rebuild path with a deterministic embedder), asserting exit/output of the factored entry functions
- [x] Run `swift build && swift test` — exit 0 without GPU

## Workflow
- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
